journal_entry.description: Customer deposit received for moving services to be performed in one month
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS 15, revenue is recognized when performance obligations are satisfied. Since the moving services will be performed in one month, the $5,000 deposit received creates a liability (unearned revenue/customer deposit) until the services are delivered. The cash received increases the asset account. No tax is recorded at this point because the deposit represents a prepayment for future services; tax will be recognized when the revenue is earned and the services are performed.

output.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit, Atlas debits its bank/chequing account (asset increase). The deposit creates a liability (unearned revenue/customer deposit) which is credited, but we only classify the debit side here.

debit_classifier.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit, Atlas debits its bank/chequing account (asset increase). The deposit creates a liability (unearned revenue/customer deposit) which is credited, but we only classify the debit side here.

output.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit, Atlas has two credit-side effects: (1) Cash/chequing account increases = asset increase, but we classify CREDITS only, so this is on the debit side. (2) The deposit creates an obligation to perform services in the future = unearned revenue/deferred revenue, which is a liability increase. Therefore, credit side shows one liability increase line.

credit_classifier.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit, Atlas has two credit-side effects: (1) Cash/chequing account increases = asset increase, but we classify CREDITS only, so this is on the debit side. (2) The deposit creates an obligation to perform services in the future = unearned revenue/deferred revenue, which is a liability increase. Therefore, credit side shows one liability increase line.
